claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child or children you'd like to claim for.**

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please let me know about each of them and I can assess their eligibility individually.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 7 years old, so under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information about Alex! Since he's 7 years old (under 16), that's a great start for eligibility. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Since Alex is only 7 years old, I assume he's not working. Can you confirm that Alex is **not** working 24 or more hours per week in paid employment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's not working at all - he's only 7!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving any of the following benefits in his own right?**
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

These are benefits that, if a child receives them directly, would affect eligibility. Given Alex's age (7 years old), it's very unlikely he would be receiving these benefits himself. Can you confirm Alex is not receiving ESA or Universal Credit in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

At 7 years old, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm. Is Alex:

- **Not in any apprenticeship** (most likely at his age!)
- In a **Foundation Apprenticeship in Wales** (which would still be fine)
- In a **disqualifying apprenticeship** (such as an Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, or Degree Apprenticeship in England, a Modern Apprenticeship in Scotland, or a non-Foundation apprenticeship in Wales)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Primary school at age 7 is exactly right. Let me move on to the next part of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of these applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim as a jobseeker for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and family (and not claiming certain benefits like Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK actually. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you mentioned you're living abroad, I have a follow-up question:

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

Crown servants include people such as:
- UK government employees working overseas (e.g., diplomats, civil servants posted abroad)
- Members of the armed forces stationed overseas
- Those working for certain UK-funded organisations abroad

Or are you living in certain qualifying countries under specific circumstances?

If none of these apply and you simply live abroad without any of the special circumstances listed in my previous message, unfortunately that would affect your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Could you tell me a bit more about your situation abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I just live abroad with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. I'm sorry, but based on what you've told me, I need to navigate to the outcome for residency requirements not being met.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I've now completed the assessment for Alex. Let me provide you with the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry to inform you that based on our assessment, you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why?

While Alex meets all the child-related criteria:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in his own name
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship

**The issue is with the residency requirement:**
- ❌ You do not normally live in the UK
- ❌ You are not a Crown servant living abroad
- ❌ You don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

### What are your options?

1. **If you move back to the UK:** You would be able to apply for Child Benefit once you are normally resident in the UK again.

2. **If your circumstances change:** For example, if you become a Crown servant or obtain settled/pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, you may become eligible.

3. **Check for other support:** Depending on which country you live in, there may be local family benefits or child support schemes available to you there.

I understand this isn't the news you were hoping for. If you have any questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please don't hesitate to ask for another assessment.

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own name, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally qualify him as a child for Child Benefit purposes. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying situation (such as having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme). UK residency requirements must be met by the claimant to receive Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, be a Crown servant living abroad, or live in certain qualifying countries. None of these apply in this case.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant (parent) does not meet UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying situation (such as having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme). UK residency requirements must be met by the claimant to receive Child Benefit.

To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, be a Crown servant living abroad, or live in certain qualifying countries.
